998	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — slice-to-geometric presentation transport (gap1, P1)
999	
1000	This section builds the geometric milestone of the gap1 per-element transport
1001	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1002	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` is transported, across the slice-to-geometric
1003	bridge `overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C), into a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the *geometric*
1004	restriction `(Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
1005	
1006	The load-bearing ingredient is the unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso`: the slice-to-geometric
1007	equivalence functor `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor` (definitionally a `SheafOfModules.pushforward`
1008	along the equivalence-of-sites inverse with the *identity* ring comparison) sends the
1009	structure-sheaf module `unit` to `unit`. This is exactly the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum that
1010	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` +
1011	`Presentation.ofIsIso` (across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests
1012	on the general fact `isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map
1013	`unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an iso whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is
1014	(here `ψ = 𝟙`).
1015	
1016	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit` and proves it iso
1017	only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
1018	`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the slice transport, are
1019	project-local. -/
1020	
1021	section SliceGeometricPresentation
1022	
1023	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1024	
1025	/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**
1026	
1027	For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
1028	`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical map
1029	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its
1030	component on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, iso as `ψ` is;
1031	the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
1032	`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.
1033	
1034	Project-local: Mathlib proves `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` iso only under a finality hypothesis on `F`
1035	(`SheafOfModules.PullbackFree`); this `IsIso ψ`-driven form is the one the slice-to-geometric
1036	unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso` (gap1, P1) consumes (with `ψ = 𝟙`). -/
1037	private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
1038	    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1039	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
1040	    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
1041	    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
1042	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1043	    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1044	    (hψ : IsIso ψ) :
1045	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ) := by
1046	  haveI := hψ
1047	  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
1048	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
1049	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
1050	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
1051	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
1052	  intro V
1053	  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
1054	  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
1055	  convert this using 1
1056	
1057	namespace Scheme.Modules
1058	
1059	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1060	
1061	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).
1062	
1063	For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
1064	(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
1065	identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
1066	structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
1067	`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
1068	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
1069	noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
1070	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
1071	      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
1072	  unfold overRestrictEquiv
1073	  dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
1074	  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
1075	      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
1076	      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
1077	      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
1078	    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
1079	  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))
1080	
1081	/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).
1082	
1083	Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1084	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1085	*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
1086	transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
1087	`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge
1088	`overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C).
1089	
1090	This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport
1091	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): with `U = q.X i` and
1092	`P = q.presentation i` it produces a global presentation of `U.ι^* M`; the remaining geometric step
1093	restricts further to a basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1094	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. Project-local. -/
1095	noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
1096	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
1097	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
1098	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))
1099	
1100	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1101	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1102	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1103	/-- **Geometric restriction to a cover member is globally presented** (gap1, P1).
1104	
1105	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q` and an index `i`, the geometric
1106	restriction `(pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M = (q.X i).ι^* M` of `M` to the open subscheme
1107	`(q.X i).toScheme` admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation`. It is `overRestrictPresentation` applied
1108	to the slice presentation `q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation` supplied by the
1109	quasi-coherence datum.
1110	
1111	This is the per-cover-member output that feeds the affine descent of the gap1 transport
1112	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): for `D(r) ≤ q.X i` one further restricts this
1113	presentation to the basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1114	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. The heartbeat headroom tames the slice-site
1115	`IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` synthesis blow-up that `Presentation.map` triggers across the
1116	equivalence functor (the same `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` incantation Mathlib's own
1117	`QuasicoherentData.bind` uses). Project-local. -/
1118	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData (M : X.Modules)
1119	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) :
1120	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M).Presentation :=
1121	  overRestrictPresentation (q.X i) M (q.presentation i)
1122	
1123	end Scheme.Modules
1124	
1125	end SliceGeometricPresentation

1127	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — basic-open presentation descent (gap1, P1 keystone)
1128	
1129	This section assembles the gap1 per-element keystone
1130	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`
1131	(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`): on a basic open `D(r)` contained in a cover member
1132	`q.X i` of the quasi-coherence data, the restricted sheaf `M|_{D(r)}` is a geometric tilde, i.e.
1133	its `fromTildeΓ` counit is an isomorphism.
1134	
1135	The route follows the affine descent of the recipe, building on the slice-to-geometric presentation
1136	transport of the previous section:
1137	
1138	1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* `Presentation` of the geometric
1139	   restriction `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the open subscheme `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
1140	2. For any open `W ⊆ Z`, the global presentation `PN` slices to a slice presentation `N.over W` via
1141	   the single `Presentation.map` of the over-functor `pushforward (𝟙 …)` (the
1142	   `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template — no iterated-slice equivalence is needed because
1143	   `PN` is already a global presentation on the genuine scheme `Z`).
1144	3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` transports it to a global presentation of the geometric
1145	   restriction `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1146	4. For `W` *affine*, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` identifies `W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)`; transporting the
1147	   presentation across this iso (whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso)
1148	   lands a global presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`.
1149	5. A global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism
1150	   (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
1151	
1152	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge; this descent
1153	is project-local. -/
1154	
1155	section BasicOpenPresentationDescent
1156	
1157	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1158	
1159	namespace Scheme.Modules
1160	
1161	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1162	
1163	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1164	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1165	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1166	/-- **Presentation of the geometric restriction of `M` to an open `W` of a cover member** (gap1, P1).
1167	
1168	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and *any* open
1169	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction
1170	`(pullback W.ι).obj ((pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M)` of `M` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`,
1171	then to `W`) admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1172	
1173	It slices the *global* presentation `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` of
1174	`N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on `Z` down to the slice `N.over W` (a single `Presentation.map` of the
1175	over-functor — the `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template, no iterated-slice equivalence
1176	needed since `PN` is global on the genuine scheme `Z`), then geometrizes via
1177	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local: feeds the affine descent of the gap1 keystone
1178	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`. -/
1179	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιRestrict (M : X.Modules)
1180	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) :
1181	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1182	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)).Presentation :=
1183	  overRestrictPresentation W ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)
1184	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u}
1185	      (presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i)
1186	      (SheafOfModules.pushforward
1187	        (𝟙 ((show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf.over W))) (by rfl))
1188	
1189	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is an equivalence of opens sites.** For `φ : Y ≅ Z`,
1190	the inverse-image functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens ↥Y ⥤ Opens ↥Z` is an equivalence (with
1191	inverse `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), assembled from the pseudofunctoriality isos `Opens.mapComp` /
1192	`Opens.mapId`. Its purpose is to supply the `Final` instance that makes `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an
1193	isomorphism in `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso`. Project-local. -/
1194	noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1195	    TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Z where
1196	  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
1197	  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base
1198	  unitIso := (Opens.mapId _).symm ≪≫
1199	      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
1200	        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
1201	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1202	      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base
1203	  counitIso := (Opens.mapComp φ.inv.base φ.hom.base).symm ≪≫
1204	      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
1205	        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
1206	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1207	      Opens.mapId _
1208	
1209	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is final.** Immediate from
1210	`opensMapEquivOfIso` (an equivalence is final); the `Final` fact needed by
1211	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. Supplied via `haveI` at use sites (instance resolution cannot invert
1212	`φ.inv.base`). Project-local. -/
1213	theorem opensMap_final_of_schemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1214	    (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := by
1215	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).IsEquivalence := (opensMapEquivOfIso φ).isEquivalence_functor
1216	  infer_instance
1217	
1218	/-- **Pullback along an iso of schemes sends the unit module to the unit module** (gap1, P1).
1219	
1220	For an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z`, the pullback functor along `φ.inv : Z ⟶ Y` carries the
1221	structure-sheaf (unit) module of `Y` to that of `Z`. The underlying canonical comparison
1222	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism because the site functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base` of an iso
1223	of schemes is `Final` (`opensMap_final_of_schemeIso`). This is the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum
1224	consumed by `Presentation.map` along `pullback φ.inv` in `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`.
1225	Project-local. -/
1226	noncomputable def pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1227	    (SheafOfModules.pullback (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).obj (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1228	      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf := by
1229	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := opensMap_final_of_schemeIso φ
1230	  haveI : (SheafOfModules.pushforward (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).IsRightAdjoint := inferInstance
1231	  exact asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom))
1232	
1233	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1234	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1235	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1236	/-- **A presentation transports across the pullback by an iso of schemes** (gap1, P1, step 4).
1237	
1238	Given an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z` and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of a module `N` on
1239	`Y`, the geometric pullback `(pullback φ.inv).obj N` of `N` to `Z` admits a presentation. It is
1240	`Presentation.map` along the colimit-preserving pullback functor `pullback φ.inv`, using the unit-iso
1241	`pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ`. This is the affine-identification transport step of the gap1 keystone:
1242	applied with `φ` the `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` of the affine restriction, it moves the presentation onto
1243	a genuine `Spec`. Project-local. -/
1244	noncomputable def presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z)
1245	    (N : Y.Modules) (P : N.Presentation) :
1246	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj N).Presentation :=
1247	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1} (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv) :=
1248	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.inv).leftAdjoint_preservesColimits
1249	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv)
1250	    (pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ)
1251	
1252	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1253	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1254	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1255	/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on every affine open of a cover member** (gap1, P1).
1256	
1257	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and an *affine* open
1258	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine
1259	`Spec Γ((q.X i).toScheme, W) ≅ W` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, then to `W`, then across
1260	the affine identification `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`) has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit — i.e. it is
1261	a geometric tilde.
1262	
1263	This is the geometric heart of the gap1 per-element transport: the slice presentation supplied by
1264	the quasi-coherence datum geometrizes (`presentationPullbackιRestrict`) to a global presentation on
1265	`W.toScheme`, which transports across the affine iso (`presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`) to a global
1266	presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`; a global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an
1267	isomorphism (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`). Project-local: Mathlib has no
1268	`QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge. -/
1269	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback (M : X.Modules)
1270	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I)
1271	    (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) (hW : IsAffineOpen W) :
1272	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hW.isoSpec.inv).obj
1273	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1274	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))).fromTildeΓ :=
1275	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation _
1276	    (presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso hW.isoSpec
1277	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1278	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))
1279	      (presentationPullbackιRestrict M q i W))
1280	
1281	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1282	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1283	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1284	/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover** (gap1, P1 keystone,
1285	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`).
1286	
1287	Let `M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` with quasi-coherence data `q`, and let `r : R` with
1288	`D(r) ≤ q.X i` for some cover member. Then the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine basic open
1289	`D(r)` — realised as the preimage `W := (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` inside the cover-member subscheme
1290	`Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, transported across the affine identification `W ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)` (which is
1291	`Spec R_r` since `D(r)` is affine) — has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit, i.e. `M|_{D(r)}` is a
1292	geometric tilde.
1293	
1294	This is the per-element step of gap1: it is the affine instance `W = (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` of
1295	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback`, with affineness of `W` from
1296	`IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen` (`D(r)` is affine in `Spec R`) and
1297	`IsAffineOpen.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion` (its preimage under the open immersion `(q.X i).ι` is
1298	affine, using `D(r) ≤ q.X i = (q.X i).ι.opensRange`). Project-local. -/
1299	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1300	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (r : R) (i : q.I)
1301	    (hr : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i) :
1302	    IsIso (@Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
1303	      (Γ(↑(q.X i), (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)))

1629	    (Hfr : ∀ U : (Spec R).Opens, (∃ r ∈ t, U ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) →
1630	      IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
1631	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
1632	          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ U ≤ U)).op).hom) :
1633	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
1634	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
1635	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom where
1636	  map_units := map_units_restrict_basicOpen M f
1637	  surj y := by
1638	    obtain ⟨x, n, hxn⟩ := descent_surj M f t hspan (fun U _ hcov => Hfr U hcov) y

1641	    have hθ : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
1642	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom (x₁ - x₂) = 0 := by
1643	      rw [map_sub, h, sub_self]
1644	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := descent_smul_eq_zero M f t hspan
1645	      (fun r hr => Hfr (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) ⟨r, hr, le_refl _⟩) (x₁ - x₂) hθ

1	\chapter{The Quot scheme}
2	\label{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}

5	% STRATEGY NOTE. This chapter packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
6	% Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
7	% \(\mathrm{Quot}^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\): the per-fiber Hilbert polynomial, the
8	% Quot functor of \(T\)-flat coherent quotients of \(E_T\) on
9	% \(X_T = X \times_S T\), and the Grassmannian \emph{scheme} together with
10	% its representability. The Quot functor underlies the relative Picard
11	% functor via the Hilbert scheme \(\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}\)
12	% -- the case \(E = \mathcal{O}_X\) of the Quot functor.
13	%
14	% LOAD-BEARING SUB-PREREQUISITE. The Quot construction embeds Quot as a
15	% locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian
16	% \(\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))\).
17	% Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} -- only
18	% a linear-algebra Grassmannian as a finite-rank-quotient variety. The
19	% scheme-level Grassmannian over an arbitrary noetherian base is therefore an
20	% in-project sub-build. This chapter introduces it as
21	% \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} and states its representability
22	% (\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).
23	
24	\section{Setup and motivation}
25	\label{sec:quot_setup}
26	
27	Throughout this chapter, let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a
28	projective morphism, \(L\) a relatively very ample line bundle on \(X\) over \(S\),
29	and \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\). The Quot construction packages the
30	\(T\)-flat coherent quotients \(E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of the
31	pull-back \(E_T\) to \(X_T = X \times_S T\), modulo the equivalence
32	\(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) defined by
33	\(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). Fixing the Hilbert polynomial
34	\(\Phi(\lambda) = \chi(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}(\lambda)) \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
35	(constant on connected \(T\) by flatness)
36	stratifies the Quot functor into pieces \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\), each of
37	which Grothendieck proved is representable by a projective \(S\)-scheme.
38	This chapter develops the foundations of that picture: the Hilbert
39	polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}), the Quot functor
40	(\cref{def:quot_functor}), and the Grassmannian scheme
41	(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) with its representability
42	(\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).
43	
44	% SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1;
45	% FGA Explained Ch.~5, AMS MSM 123 (read from
46	% references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L287-L500).
47	% The Hartshorne reference for the polynomial-eventually
48	% property of Euler characteristics is [Hartshorne], III.5.2 (the scanned
49	% PDF at references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf has no text layer, so
50	% the verbatim source-quote is taken from
51	% Nitsure \S 1; Hartshorne III.5.2 is cited in prose as the textbook
52	% reference).
53	
54	\section{Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf}
55	\label{sec:hilbert_polynomial}
56	
57	\begin{definition}\leanok
58	[Hilbert polynomial]
59	  \label{def:hilbert_polynomial}
60	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial}
61	  \uses{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}
62	  % ENCODING. `def:hilbert_polynomial` is formalized via the GRADED HILBERT
63	  % FUNCTION of the section module ⊕_{m≥0} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})
64	  % (def:sectionGradedModule), whose polynomiality is extracted in
65	  % thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule from the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality
66	  % lemma (lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational) together with the Mathlib lemma
67	  % Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly (lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib). The
68	  % construction uses only H^0; for m≫0 it agrees with the Euler characteristic
69	  % χ by Serre vanishing, so it is the classical Hilbert polynomial and no
70	  % numerical invariant is lost.
71	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
72	  % Polynomials" (read from
73	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L478).
74	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
75	  % "Let \(X\) be a finite type scheme over
76	  %  a field \(k\), together with a line bundle \(L\).
77	  %  Recall that if \(F\) is a coherent sheaf on
78	  %  \(X\) whose support is proper over \(k\), then
79	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\) of \(F\) is defined by
80	  %  the function
81	  %  \(\){\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
82	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})\(\)
83	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
84	  %  coherence and properness conditions. The fact that \(\chi(F(m))\)
85	  %  is indeed a polynomial in \(m\) under the above assumption is a special
86	  %  case of what is known as Snapper's Lemma (see Kleiman [K] for a proof).
87	  %
88	  %  Let \(X\to S\) be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
89	  %  and let \(L\) be a line bundle on \(X\). Let \(\F\)
90	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
91	  %  \(X\) whose schematic support is proper over \(S\). Then for each \(s\in S\),
92	  %  we get a polynomial \({\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]\)
93	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
94	  %  the restriction \(\F_s = F|_{X_s}\) of \(\F\) to the fiber \(X_s\) over \(s\),
95	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle \(L_s = L|_{X_s}\).
96	  %  If \(\F\) is flat over \(S\) then the function \(s\mapsto {\Phi}_s\)
97	  %  from the set of points of \(S\) to the polynomial ring \(\Q[\lambda]\) is
98	  %  known to be locally constant on \(S\)."
99	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
100	  III.5.2.}
101	  Let \(X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism of noetherian schemes and let \(L\)
102	  be a line bundle on \(X\). For a coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) whose
103	  schematic support is proper over \(S\) and a point \(s \in S\), write \(X_s\)
104	  for the fibre over \(s\), \(\mathcal{F}_s = \mathcal{F}|_{X_s}\) and
105	  \(L_s = L|_{X_s}\). The \emph{Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}\) at \(s\)
106	  relative to \(L\)} is the unique polynomial
107	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\) that agrees, for all
108	  \(m \gg 0\), with the graded Hilbert function
109	  \[
110	    m \;\longmapsto\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr)
111	  \]
112	  of the section module
113	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
114	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}), viewed as a finitely generated graded module
115	  over the homogeneous section ring
116	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
117	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}). Existence and uniqueness of
118	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) are the content of
119	  \cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}.
120	
121	  This is the classical Hilbert polynomial. When \(\mathcal{F}_s\) is coherent
122	  and \(L_s\) is ample on the proper \(\kappa(s)\)-scheme \(X_s\), Serre vanishing
123	  gives \(H^i(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}) = 0\) for all \(i > 0\)
124	  and \(m \gg 0\), so for \(m \gg 0\)
125	  \[
126	    \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
127	    \;=\; \chi(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
128	    \;=\; \sum_{i \ge 0} (-1)^i \dim_{\kappa(s)} H^i(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}),
129	  \]
130	  and hence \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) coincides with the Euler-characteristic
131	  Hilbert polynomial of Snapper's Lemma -- no numerical invariant is lost. The
132	  construction above, however, uses only the global sections \(H^0\): the higher
133	  cohomology enters solely through the classical statement that the two functions
134	  agree for \(m \gg 0\), never through the definition of \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\)
135	  itself. When \(\mathcal{F}\) is \(S\)-flat the function
136	  \(s \mapsto \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is locally constant on \(S\), so \(\Phi\) is
137	  well-defined on connected components.
138	\end{definition}

140	\section{Graded Hilbert polynomial}
141	\label{sec:graded_hilbert_polynomial}
142	
143	This section gives the graded-Hilbert-function construction that underlies
144	\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Working over a single fibre \(X_s\) with its
145	ample line bundle \(L_s\) and coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}_s\), it builds the
146	section graded ring (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) and module
147	(\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}), records their finite generation
148	(\cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}), and extracts the Hilbert polynomial
149	(\cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}) by combining the graded
150	Hilbert--Serre rationality of the Hilbert series
151	(\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}) with Mathlib's polynomial-extraction
152	lemma (\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}). The route uses only \(H^0\)
153	(global sections), so it is independent of higher coherent cohomology.
154	
155	\begin{definition}
156	[Section graded ring]
157	  \label{def:sectionGradedRing}
158	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedRing}
159	  % NOTE: Formalization is blocked on the absence of tensor products for sheaves of modules in
160	  % Mathlib at the pinned commit. The degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
161	  % requires a tensor power \(L_s^{\otimes m}\) of a line bundle; this infrastructure (tensor
162	  % product of sheaves of modules with lax-monoidal global sections) is not yet available, making
163	  % this definition a prerequisite sub-build.
164	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
165	  % sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert Polynomials" (read from
166	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
167	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
168	  % "Let $X$ be a finite type scheme over
169	  %  a field $k$, together with a line bundle $L$.
170	  %  Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
171	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
172	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
173	  %  the function
174	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
175	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
176	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
177	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
178	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); the section graded
179	  ring is the homogeneous coordinate ring of [Hartshorne], I.7.}
180	  Let \(s \in S\) and let \(L_s\) be a line bundle on the fibre \(X_s\), a scheme
181	  of finite type over the residue field \(\kappa(s)\). The \emph{section graded
182	  ring} of \(L_s\) is the graded commutative ring
183	  \[
184	    R(X_s, L_s) \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
185	  \]
186	  with degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\) and multiplication
187	  the tensor product of sections
188	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
189	  \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m'}) \to \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\),
190	  \(\sigma \otimes \tau \mapsto \sigma \cdot \tau\). Its degree-zero component is
191	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{O}_{X_s})\), which contains \(\kappa(s)\). When \(X_s\) is
192	  proper over \(\kappa(s)\) and \(L_s\) is ample, \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely
193	  generated -- hence Noetherian -- graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra (a suitable
194	  Veronese power of \(L_s\) is very ample and embeds \(X_s\) into a projective
195	  space, exhibiting \(R(X_s, L_s)\) up to a finite extension as a quotient of a
196	  polynomial ring).
197	\end{definition}

1705	/-- **(I) Ring-iso-semilinear `IsLocalizedModule` transport.** Given a ring isomorphism
1706	`σ : R ≃+* R'`, two `σ`-semilinear additive isomorphisms `e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁`, `e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂` (i.e.
1707	`eᵢ (a • x) = σ a • eᵢ x`), and an `R'`-linear map `h : N₁ →ₗ[R'] N₂` intertwining a localization

1711	`R`-linear `Hfr` data. Project-local. -/
1712	theorem isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear
1713	    {R R' : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing R'] (σ : R ≃+* R')
1714	    {M₁ M₂ N₁ N₂ : Type*}
1715	    [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module R M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂]
1716	    [AddCommGroup N₁] [Module R' N₁] [AddCommGroup N₂] [Module R' N₂]
1717	    (S : Submonoid R)
1718	    (g : M₁ →ₗ[R] M₂) [IsLocalizedModule S g]
1719	    (e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁) (e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂)
1720	    (he₁ : ∀ (a : R) (x : M₁), e₁ (a • x) = σ a • e₁ x)
1721	    (he₂ : ∀ (a : R) (x : M₂), e₂ (a • x) = σ a • e₂ x)
1722	    (h : N₁ →ₗ[R'] N₂)
1723	    (hh : ∀ x, h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)) :
1724	    IsLocalizedModule (S.map (σ : R →+* R')) h where
1725	  map_units x := by
1726	    obtain ⟨s, hs, hsx⟩ := x.2
1727	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1728	    have hsrc := IsLocalizedModule.map_units g ⟨s, hs⟩
1729	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hsrc

1733	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Function.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply,
1734	        Module.algebraMap_end_apply, he₂, e₂.apply_symm_apply]
1735	      congr 1
1736	      exact hsx.symm
1737	    rw [hfun]
1738	    exact e₂.bijective.comp (hsrc.comp e₂.symm.bijective)
1739	  surj y := by
1740	    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hx⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj S g (e₂.symm y)
1741	    refine ⟨⟨e₁ x, ⟨σ ↑s, ↑s, s.2, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1742	    have he : e₂ ((↑s : R) • e₂.symm y) = e₂ (g x) := congrArg e₂ hx
1743	    rw [he₂, e₂.apply_symm_apply, ← hh] at he

1745	  exists_of_eq {y₁ y₂} heq := by

1747	      rw [← hh, ← hh, e₁.apply_symm_apply, e₁.apply_symm_apply]; exact heq

1750	    have hc' : (↑c : R) • e₁.symm y₁ = (↑c : R) • e₁.symm y₂ := hc
1751	    have hcc := congrArg e₁ hc'
1752	    rw [he₁, he₁, e₁.apply_symm_apply, e₁.apply_symm_apply] at hcc
1753	    exact hcc

1756	`Rr`-linear and exhibits a localization at `powers (algebraMap R Rr f)` over a base-changed ring

1765	    [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂] [Module Rr M₂] [IsScalarTower R Rr M₂]
1766	    (f : R) (g : M₁ →ₗ[Rr] M₂)
1767	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f)) g] :
1768	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (g.restrictScalars R) where

1770	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := x.2
1771	    have hn' : f ^ n = ↑x := hn
1772	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1773	    have hmem : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n ∈ Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f) := ⟨n, rfl⟩

1784	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
1785	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑s := hn
1786	    refine ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1787	    have hsmul : (f ^ n : R) • y = (↑s : Rr) • y := by
1788	      rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]

1790	    rw [hsmul, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars]; exact hx

1793	      (f := g) heq

1795	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑c := hn
1796	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
1797	    have e1 : (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (↑c : Rr) • x₁ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]

1802	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D Hfr: pullback-section transport
1803	
1804	The remaining gated ingredient of the named gap1 keystone
1805	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` is the **section-level** analogue of P1's object-level
1806	transport: for an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y` and a sheaf of modules `M` on `Y`, the sections of the
1807	geometric pullback `(pullback f).obj M` over an open `U ⊆ X` are canonically identified with the
1808	sections of `M` over the image `f ''ᵁ U`.

1810	The construction is `Γ(-, U)` applied to the inverse of Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback f`
1811	(`restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f`) at `M`, using the *definitional* identity
1812	`Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` (`Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`, `rfl`). Because
1813	both sides are `Γ(-, U)` of a single fixed module isomorphism, naturality in `U` (intertwining the
1814	presheaf restriction maps) is free: it is the naturality of the underlying abelian-presheaf
1815	morphism. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit; project-bespoke. -/

1817	/-- **Global sections of a pullback along an open immersion are sections over the image**
1818	(gap1, Hfr section transport, general open). For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`, a sheaf of modules
1819	`M` on `Y`, and an open `U ⊆ X`, the additive groups of sections satisfy
1820	`Γ((pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)`. This is `Γ(-, U)` of `(restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)⁻¹`
1821	at `M`; the codomain is `Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` definitionally. Naturality
1822	in `U` is `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality`. -/

1825	    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) :=
1826	  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙ (CategoryTheory.evaluation _ _).obj (Opposite.op U)).mapIso
1827	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M)

1830	For opens `V ≤ U` of `X`, `gammaPullbackImageIso` commutes with the presheaf restriction maps of
1831	`(pullback f).obj M` and of `M` (along the image inclusion `f ''ᵁ V ≤ f ''ᵁ U`). This is the
1832	naturality of the underlying morphism of abelian presheaves. -/

1835	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op ≫ (gammaPullbackImageIso f M V).hom

1837	  exact (((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M).hom.mapPresheaf).naturality i.op

1868	    (M : Y.Modules) (V : X.Opens) (a : Γ(X, V))
1869	    (x : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj M, V)) :
1870	    (gammaPullbackImageIso j M V).hom (a • x)

1872	  -- `gammaPullbackImageIso j M V`'s forward map is `Γ(-, V)` of the `Ab`-morphism
1873	  -- `ψ := ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback j).symm.app M).hom`, i.e. the section map `ψ.app V`.

1875	    Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf_map, CategoryTheory.evaluation_obj_map,
1876	    Scheme.Modules.mapPresheaf_app]

1878	  -- `Γ(X, V)`-action being `restrictFunctor`'s `restrictScalars`-action along `(j.appIso V).inv`.
1879	  erw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul]
1880	  -- The `restrictScalars` action `a •_{restrict} m` is defeq to `(j.appIso V).inv a •_M m`,
1881	  -- and `σ_V a = gammaImageRingEquiv j V a = (j.appIso V).inv a`, so the two sides agree by `rfl`.
1882	  rfl

1886	The two `IsLocalizedModule` bridges (I) `isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` and (II)

1950	noncomputable def compositeBasicOpenImmersion {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1951	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (s : R) (i : q.I)

1956	    Scheme.Opens.ι ((Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen s)) ≫

1959	/-- **(producer, a) `fromTildeΓ` iso of the composite-immersion pullback.** For a quasi-coherent
1960	`M` on `Spec R` and a basic open `D(s) ≤ q.X i`, the pullback module `(pullback j).obj M` along the

1963	The geometric content is the P1 keystone `isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`, which supplies

1965	`(pullback isoSpec.inv).obj ((pullback ι_W).obj ((pullback ι_{q.X i}).obj M))`; the iterated and the
1966	composite pullbacks are identified by the `pullbackComp` pseudofunctor coherences, and

1969	theorem pullback_composite_immersion_isIso_fromTildeΓ {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1970	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (s : R) (i : q.I)
1971	    (hs : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen s : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i) :

2230	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap2 single-chart transport
2231	
2232	The general-scheme keystone `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`
2233	(`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`): for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M` on an *arbitrary* scheme
2234	`X`, an affine open `U`, and `f : Γ(X, U)`, the section restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, D(f))` is
2235	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X, U)`.
2236	
2237	It is the single-chart affine transport on top of G1-core: pull `M` back along the affine immersion
2238	`hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(X, U) ⟶ X` (range `U`), so the pullback `M'` is quasi-coherent on
2239	`Spec Γ(X, U)`, where gap1 gives `IsIso M'.fromTildeΓ`; the engine
2240	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ` localizes the slice restriction over `Γ(X, U)`, and
2241	the `σ`-semilinear section comparison `gammaPullbackImageIso` (bridge (I)
2242	`isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear`) transports it to the `M`-side restriction. No
2243	cover-and-glue: `U` is already affine, so there is a single chart. -/
2244	
2245	/-- **The `Γ(X,U)`-linear section restriction map of a sheaf of modules.** For `M : X.Modules` and
2246	an inclusion of opens `i : V ⟶ U`, the presheaf restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, V)` is `Γ(X, U)`-linear
2247	when `Γ(M, V)` carries the `Γ(X, U)`-module structure restricted along `X.presheaf.map i.op`
2248	(`Module.compHom`). Linearity is `Scheme.Modules.map_smul`. Project-local: the linear-map packaging
2249	of the section restriction needed to state `IsLocalizedModule` for a general scheme (Mathlib's
2250	presheaf-of-modules restriction is semilinear, not bundled this way). -/

200	[Section graded module]
201	  \label{def:sectionGradedModule}
202	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule}
203	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing}
204	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
205	  % Polynomials" (read from
206	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
207	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
208	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
209	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
210	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
211	  %  the function
212	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
213	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
214	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
215	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
216	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
217	  I.7.}
218	  Let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on the fibre \(X_s\) and \(L_s\) a
219	  line bundle on \(X_s\). The \emph{section graded module} of \(\mathcal{F}_s\)
220	  twisted by \(L_s\) is the graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module
221	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing})
222	  \[
223	    M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)
224	    \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
225	  \]
226	  with degree-\(m\) component the \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space
227	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) and scalar action given
228	  by the section tensor product
229	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
230	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m'})
231	  \to \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\). Its graded Hilbert
232	  function is \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)}
233	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\).
234	\end{definition}
235	
236	\begin{lemma}
237	[Finite generation of the section graded module]
238	  \label{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}
239	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule_fg}
240	  \uses{def:sectionGradedModule, def:sectionGradedRing}
241	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
242	  % Polynomials" (read from
243	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
244	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
245	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
246	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
247	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
248	  %  the function
249	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
250	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
251	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
252	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
253	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); Serre's theorem on
254	  ample line bundles, [Hartshorne], I.7 / II.5.}
255	  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
256	  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
257	  Then the section graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) is a
258	  Noetherian graded ring, and the section graded module
259	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}) is a finitely
260	  generated graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module. In particular each component
261	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a finite-dimensional
262	  \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space.
263	\end{lemma}
264	
265	\begin{proof}
266	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, def:sectionGradedModule}
267	  Since \(L_s\) is ample and \(X_s\) is proper over \(\kappa(s)\), a Veronese
268	  power \(L_s^{\otimes e}\) is very ample and gives a closed immersion
269	  \(X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\). By Serre's theorem the section
270	  graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded
271	  \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra, so it is Noetherian. For the coherent sheaf
272	  \(\mathcal{F}_s\), the same ampleness yields, again by Serre, that
273	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a
274	  finitely generated graded module over \(R(X_s, L_s)\): the twists
275	  \(\mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\) are globally generated for
276	  \(m \gg 0\), and the finitely many generating sections in low degrees together
277	  with the multiplication maps generate \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) over
278	  \(R(X_s, L_s)\). Finite dimensionality of each component over \(\kappa(s)\)
279	  follows from coherence and properness (finiteness of cohomology of coherent
280	  sheaves on a proper scheme over a field).
281	\end{proof}

283	\begin{lemma}[Existence and uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial of a rational
284	  graded series]
285	  \label{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
286	  \lean{Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly}
287	  \mathlibok
288	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
289	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.HilbertPoly}).}
290	  Let \(F\) be a field of characteristic zero, let \(p \in F[X]\), and let
291	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then there is a unique polynomial \(h \in F[X]\) such that
292	  for some \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) and all \(n > N\) the coefficient of \(X^n\) in the
293	  power-series expansion of \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d} \in F\llbracket X\rrbracket\)
294	  equals \(h(n)\):
295	  \[
296	    \exists!\, h \in F[X],\ \exists N,\ \forall n > N : \;
297	    \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1-X)^{-d} \bigr) \;=\; h(n).
298	  \]
299	  Equivalently, the eventual coefficient function of any rational power series
300	  with denominator a power of \(1 - X\) is, for \(n \gg 0\), given by a unique
301	  polynomial \(h\) (Mathlib's \(\mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\)). The hypothesis
302	  \([\mathrm{CharZero}\,F]\) is satisfied by \(F = \mathbb{Q}\), which is the
303	  instance used to obtain \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\).
304	\end{lemma}

306	\begin{lemma}[Additivity of \(\dim_\kappa\) on a short exact sequence
307	  (rank--nullity)]
308	  \label{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}
309	  \lean{Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank}
310	  \mathlibok
311	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
312	  (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.RankNullity}).}
313	  Let \(\kappa\) be a field, \(V\) a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space and
314	  \(W \subseteq V\) a subspace. Then
315	  \[
316	    \dim_\kappa (V / W) + \dim_\kappa W \;=\; \dim_\kappa V .
317	  \]
318	  Equivalently, \(\dim_\kappa = \operatorname{finrank}_\kappa\) is additive on a
319	  short exact sequence \(0 \to W \to V \to V/W \to 0\) of finite-dimensional
320	  \(\kappa\)-vector spaces. This is the additivity that converts the degreewise
321	  short exact sequences of graded components into additive relations among the
322	  coefficients of the Hilbert series.
323	\end{lemma}
324	
325	\begin{lemma}[The rational power series \((1 - X)^{-d}\)]
326	  \label{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
327	  \lean{PowerSeries.invOneSubPow}
328	  \mathlibok
329	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
330	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown}).}
331	  Let \(S\) be a commutative ring and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then \((1 - X)^d\) is a
332	  unit in the power series ring \(S\llbracket X\rrbracket\), and Mathlib names its
333	  inverse
334	  \[
335	    \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(S, d) \;=\; (1 - X)^{-d}
336	      \;\in\; S\llbracket X\rrbracket^{\times}.
337	  \]
338	  Together with the coercion
339	  \(\operatorname{toPowerSeries} : F[X] \to F\llbracket X\rrbracket\) of a
340	  polynomial to its power series, this is the data in which a rational Hilbert
341	  series \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\) is expressed; it is the same rational-series shape
342	  whose eventual coefficients are extracted by
343	  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}.
344	\end{lemma}

346	\begin{lemma}\leanok
347	[Graded Hilbert--Serre: rationality of the Hilbert series]
348	  \label{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}
349	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational}
350	  \uses{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib,
351	    def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero, lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq,
352	    lem:ratHilb_bump, def:graded_subquotientHilb,
353	    lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}
354	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", \S "Noetherian graded
355	  % rings", Proposition \texttt{proposition-graded-hilbert-polynomial} (Tag
356	  % 00K1) (read from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13893-L13905).
357	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
358	  % "Suppose that $S$ is a Noetherian graded ring
359	  %  and $M$ a finite graded $S$-module. Consider the
360	  %  function
361	  %  $$
362	  %  \mathbf{Z} \longrightarrow K'_0(S_0), \quad
363	  %  n \longmapsto [M_n]
364	  %  $$
365	  %  see Lemma \ref{lemma-graded-module-fg}.
366	  %  If $S_{+}$ is generated by elements of degree $1$,
367	  %  then this function is a numerical polynomial."
368	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", Tag 00K1
369	  (proposition on graded Hilbert polynomials); cf.\ [Nitsure], \S 1 (Snapper's
370	  Lemma), [Hartshorne], I.7.}
371	  Let \(R\) be a Noetherian graded ring whose degree-zero part is a field
372	  \(\kappa\) and which is generated as a \(\kappa\)-algebra by finitely many
373	  elements, and let \(M\) be a finitely generated graded \(R\)-module with
374	  finite-dimensional graded components \(M_n\). Then there exist
375	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N \in \mathbb{N}\)
376	  such that for all \(n > N\)
377	  \[
378	    \dim_{\kappa} M_n \;=\; \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\bigr);
379	  \]
380	  that is, the Hilbert series \(\sum_{n \ge 0} (\dim_\kappa M_n)\, X^n\) is, up to
381	  a polynomial correction in low degrees, the rational function
382	  \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\), expressed in the power-series form
383	  \(\operatorname{toPowerSeries}(p) \cdot \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, d)\)
384	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}).
385	
386	  \emph{Status.} This is the substantive half of the graded Hilbert--Serre
387	  theorem (rationality of the Hilbert series of a finitely generated graded
388	  module). It is not covered by Mathlib: \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
389	  supplies only the converse polynomial-extraction step from a rational series, not
390	  the production of the rational series from the module. The argument is realised by
391	  an \emph{ambient subquotient induction} (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}):
392	  the whole induction ranges over pairs of homogeneous submodules \(N' \le N\) of the
393	  single fixed graded module \(M\), and the Hilbert function is the ambient difference
394	  \(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa(N \cap M_n) - \dim_\kappa(N' \cap M_n)\). The class of such
395	  pairs is closed under the kernel and cokernel of multiplication by a degree-one
396	  element (each presented again as an ambient subquotient pair), so the inductive step
397	  never forms a graded quotient ring \(R/(x)\), a graded quotient module \(M/xM\), or a
398	  regrading of a derived carrier. The genuine Mathlib gap is thereby \emph{avoided}
399	  rather than filled; the residual obligation is the short ambient bookkeeping of the
400	  subquotient blocks (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb},
401	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker},
402	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff},
403	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer},
404	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}), each of which manipulates only ambient
405	  families \(n \mapsto N_{\mathrm{aux}} \cap M_n\) of submodules of \(M\).
406	  % NOTE: The power-series rationality toolkit (\cref{subsec:isRatHilb}) is formalized
407	  %   and axiom-clean; the present theorem is a closed, axiom-clean Lean declaration
408	  %   (gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational). The ambient subquotient route
409	  %   (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}) sidesteps missing graded-quotient API by
410	  %   working with ambient families of submodules of \(M\).
411	\end{lemma}

413	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1, proof, inductive step (read
414	% from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13938-L13947).
415	% "Let $\overline{M} = M/xM$. Note that the map $x : M \to M$
416	%  is {\it not} a map of graded $S$-modules, since it does
417	%  not map $M_d$ into $M_d$. Namely, for each $d$ we have the
418	%  following short exact sequence
419	%  $$
420	%  0 \to M_d \xrightarrow{x} M_{d + 1} \to \overline{M}_{d + 1} \to 0
421	%  $$
422	%  This proves that $[M_{d + 1}] - [M_d] = [\overline{M}_{d + 1}]$.
423	%  Hence we win by Lemma \ref{lemma-numerical-polynomial}."
424	\begin{proof}
425	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero, lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq,
426	    lem:ratHilb_bump, lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff,
427	    def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
428	    lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff,
429	    lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer, lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb,
430	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
431	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq, lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib,
432	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib,
433	    lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}
434	    \leanok
435	  We argue with the abstract graded \(\kappa\)-algebra
436	  \(R = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} R_n\), whose degree-zero part \(R_0 = \kappa\) is a
437	  field, and the finitely generated graded \(R\)-module
438	  \(M = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} M_n\) with finite-dimensional components \(M_n\);
439	  there is no appeal to any geometric realisation of \(R\) or \(M\).
440	
441	  \emph{Reduction to generation in degree \(1\).} Since \(R\) is Noetherian and
442	  finitely generated as a \(\kappa\)-algebra, it is generated by finitely many
443	  homogeneous elements of positive degree. Replacing \(R\) by a Veronese
444	  subalgebra \(R^{(e)} = \bigoplus_n R_{en}\) for a suitable \(e\) and re-grading
445	  (and \(M\) by the corresponding Veronese module), we may assume \(R\) is
446	  generated by its degree-one part, \(\kappa[R_1] = R\). The degree-one part
447	  \(R_1\) is then a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space whose \(\kappa\)-basis
448	  \(x_1, \dots, x_r\) generates \(R\) as a \(\kappa\)-algebra; \(r = \dim_\kappa R_1\)
449	  is the number on which we induct.
450	  % NOTE: The existence of finitely many degree-one generators (the number r the
451	  % induction is on) is expected to follow from the standing hypotheses --
452	  % "finitely generated κ-algebra" together with "generated in degree 1" -- so no
453	  % new hypothesis on gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational is anticipated. To be
454	  % confirmed when the prover formalizes the assembly: degree-1 generation gives
455	  % R₁ as a finite-dimensional κ-vector space spanning R as a κ-algebra, and a
456	  % κ-basis of R₁ is a finite degree-one generating set.
457	
458	  \emph{Set-up of the ambient data.} Regard \(M\) as the fixed ambient graded
459	  \(\kappa\)-module with internal decomposition \(n \mapsto \mathcal{M}_n := M_n\)
460	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}); the components are finite-dimensional. For each
461	  basis vector \(x_i \in R_1\) let \(t_i : M \to M\) be multiplication by \(x_i\), a
462	  \(\kappa\)-linear endomorphism that raises degree by one (it sends \(M_n\) into
463	  \(M_{n+1}\), \cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}). Since \(R\) is
464	  commutative the \(t_i\) pairwise commute, and they trivially preserve the pair of
465	  homogeneous submodules \((N, N') = (\top, \bot)\). Because \(R\) is generated by
466	  \(x_1, \dots, x_r\) and \(M\) is finitely generated over \(R\), \(M\) is finitely
467	  generated as a module over the polynomial ring \(\kappa[x_1, \dots, x_r]\) acting
468	  through the \(t_i\); this is the finiteness condition required below.
469	
470	  \emph{Invoking the ambient subquotient induction.} Apply
471	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb} to the pair \((N, N') = (\top, \bot)\)
472	  with the \(r\) commuting degree-\((+1)\) endomorphisms \(t_1, \dots, t_r\). It
473	  yields \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\) of order \(r\) for the ambient subquotient Hilbert
474	  function of this pair (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}),
475	  \[
476	    h(n) \;=\; \dim_\kappa(\top \cap \mathcal{M}_n) - \dim_\kappa(\bot \cap \mathcal{M}_n)
477	      \;=\; \dim_\kappa M_n - 0 \;=\; \dim_\kappa M_n .
478	  \]
479	  Hence \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa M_n,\, r)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}):
480	  there are \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\), \(d := r \in \mathbb{N}\) and \(N \in \mathbb{N}\)
481	  with
482	  \[
483	    \dim_\kappa M_n \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\bigr)
484	    \qquad (n > N),
485	  \]
486	  which is the claim, with pole order \(d = r\). The internal mechanics of
487	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb} -- the base case via
488	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero}, and the inductive step assembling the
489	  kernel/cokernel closure (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}), the degreewise
490	  difference identity (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}, built on
491	  \(D_5\), \cref{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}) and the finiteness transfer
492	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}) into
493	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq} and \cref{lem:ratHilb_bump} -- are the Stacks~00K1
494	  induction realised entirely with ambient homogeneous submodules of \(M\).
495	\end{proof}

2663	  intro W' y hy

2724	[Inverse of the slice equivalence sends the unit module to the unit module]
2725	  \label{def:over_restrict_unit_iso_inv}
2726	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictUnitIsoInv}
2727	  \uses{def:over_restrict_equiv, lem:isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso}
2728	  \textit{The inverse of the slice equivalence functor preserves the structure-sheaf (unit)
2729	  module; dual to \cref{def:over_restrict_unit_iso}.}
2730	  Let \(V \subseteq X\) be an open subscheme. The \emph{inverse} functor of the slice
2731	  equivalence (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv}) carries the unit (structure-sheaf) module \(\mathbf{1}\)
2732	  of \(V.\mathrm{toScheme}.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}\) to the unit module of the over-site
2733	  \(X.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{over}\,V\):
2734	  \[
2735	    (\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,V).\mathrm{inverse}.\mathrm{obj}\,
2736	      \bigl(\mathbf{1}\,(V.\mathrm{toScheme}.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf})\bigr)
2737	      \;\cong\; \mathbf{1}\,(X.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{over}\,V) .
2738	  \]
2739	\end{definition}
2740	\begin{proof}
2741	  \uses{def:over_restrict_equiv, lem:isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso}
2742	  As in the forward case (\cref{def:over_restrict_unit_iso}), the inverse functor of the equivalence is
2743	  the pushforward \(\mathrm{pushforward}\,\varphi\) of sheaves of modules along an over-site/open-site
2744	  ring comparison \(\varphi\) built from the (op of the) unit isomorphism of
2745	  \((\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,V)\); since that unit is an isomorphism, \(\varphi\) is an
2746	  isomorphism. The canonical unit comparison \(\mathrm{unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\,\varphi\) is then an
2747	  isomorphism by the \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,\varphi\)-driven criterion
2748	  (\cref{lem:isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso}), and its inverse is the asserted isomorphism.

2759	  pullback \((V.\iota^{*})\,M\) yields a presentation of the abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction
2760	  \(M.\mathrm{over}\,V\):
2761	  \[
2762	    \bigl((V.\iota^{*})\,M\bigr).\mathrm{Presentation}

2768	  \uses{def:over_restrict_unit_iso_inv, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib}
2769	  First transport the given presentation of \((V.\iota^{*})\,M\) across the inverse of the pullback

2771	  (M.\mathrm{over}\,V) \cong (V.\iota^{*})\,M\) (\cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}) via
2772	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.ofIsIso}\), obtaining a presentation of the transported slice module
2773	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,V).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,V)\). Then apply
2774	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\) (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the inverse functor

2780	  (\(\mathrm{inverse} \circ \mathrm{functor} \cong \mathrm{id}\)) via \(\mathrm{Presentation.ofIsIso}\),
2781	  giving a presentation of \(M.\mathrm{over}\,V\).

2784	\begin{definition}\leanok
2785	[Presentation of the pullback's geometric restriction to a preimage cover member]
2786	  \label{def:presentation_pullback_iota_preimage}
2787	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presentationPullbackιPreimage}
2788	  \uses{def:presentation_pullback_iota_of_quasicoherentData, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
2789	    lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}
2790	  \textit{For an open immersion $g$, the pulled-back sheaf has a presentation on the geometric
2791	  restriction to each preimage cover member.}
2792	  Let \(g : Y \to X\) be an open immersion, \(M\) a sheaf of modules on \(X\) with quasi-coherence data
2793	  \(q\), and \(i\) a cover index. Write \(N := (\mathrm{pullback}\,g).\mathrm{obj}\,M\) and
2794	  \(W_i := g^{-1}(q.X_i)\). Then the geometric restriction
2795	  \((W_i.\iota^{*})\,N\) admits a \(\mathrm{SheafOfModules.Presentation}\) on \(W_i.\mathrm{toScheme}\).
2796	\end{definition}
2797	\begin{proof}
2798	  \uses{def:presentation_pullback_iota_of_quasicoherentData, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
2799	    lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}
2800	  The open immersion \(g\) restricts to an open immersion
2801	  \(k : W_i.\mathrm{toScheme} \to (q.X_i).\mathrm{toScheme}\) on preimages, characterized by the
2802	  commuting square \(k \circ (q.X_i).\iota = W_i.\iota \circ g\) (the morphism induced by \(g\) between
2803	  the inclusion of \(W_i = g^{-1}(q.X_i)\) and that of \(q.X_i\)). By pseudofunctoriality of pullback
2804	  (\(\mathrm{pullbackComp}\), named in prose) this square gives a natural isomorphism
2805	  \[
2806	    (W_i.\iota^{*})\,N \;=\; (W_i.\iota^{*})\,(g^{*}M)
2807	      \;\cong\; (W_i.\iota \circ g)^{*}M
2808	      \;=\; (k \circ (q.X_i).\iota)^{*}M
2809	      \;\cong\; k^{*}\bigl((q.X_i).\iota^{*}\,M\bigr) .
2810	  \]
2811	  Now \((q.X_i).\iota^{*}\,M\) carries the geometric presentation
2812	  \cref{def:presentation_pullback_iota_of_quasicoherentData}. Apply \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\)
2813	  (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the pullback functor \(k^{*}\)
2814	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}), which preserves colimits and carries the unit sheaf to the
2815	  unit sheaf, to obtain a presentation of \(k^{*}\bigl((q.X_i).\iota^{*}\,M\bigr)\); transport it across
2816	  the displayed isomorphism via \(\mathrm{Presentation.ofIsIso}\) to land a presentation of
2817	  \((W_i.\iota^{*})\,N\).
2818	\end{proof}
